articles.title: Gajapati district officials foil couple’s bid to marry off minor daughter
articles.description: Gajapati district administration successfully thwarted a child marriage bid in Goshani block, rescuing a minor girl scheduled to marry a man over 30. Officials intervened after receiving a tip-off, ensuring the girl's safety and providing counselling. Her parents have given a written assurance to prevent her marriage until she turns 18 and to prioritize her education.

articles.description: The UK split the Chagos archipelago from Mauritius as a result of over 150 years of British colonial rule, Roslyn Fuller, director of the non-profit think tank Solonian Democracy Institute, told Sputnik Africa.

HYBE's Poor Human Rights Assessment Score SHOCKS K-Pop World - Here's What It Means
articles.description: HYBE ranked 48th out of 50 in a human rights assessment, scoring an abysmal 2.25/12. The report flagged weak policies and zero due diligence in the company that manages K-pop group BTS.

articles.title: China Issues Urgent SOS to Nationals In Iran Amid Fears Of Resumption Of Hostilities In MidEast
articles.description: Beijing has just sent a chilling signal to the world. China’s Foreign Ministry is ordering all its nationals in Iran to relocate to "safer areas" immediately, citing a security environment that is becoming "complex and severe." This isn't just a routine travel advisory—it’s a strategic freeze on movement that often precedes massive military escalation. As Chinese citizens are told to avoid military zones at all costs, the message is clear: Beijing believes the window for safety in Tehran is rapidly closing.

articles.title: West Bengal exit polls 2026 Date and Time: Check when you can get an idea who is leading the TMC vs BJP race
articles.description: Campaigning concludes for West Bengal's final election phase as attention turns to voting and upcoming exit polls. A silence period is in effect until 6:00 PM on April 29, prohibiting election content on media platforms. Exit polls for multiple states are slated for release on April 29 after 6:30 PM, with vote counting scheduled for May 4.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by James Baldwin: “A child cannot be taught without respect” — lessons on honesty, education, trust, and emotional understanding by one of the most powerful voices on race and society in the 20th century
articles.description: James Baldwin shares a powerful idea about teaching and truth. His quote shows that children need respect and honesty to learn properly. It explains how emotions and trust play a big role in education. His words still guide teachers, parents, and young people to build better understanding and real learning.

articles.title: SAD leader Bikram Singh Majithia claims 2 AAP Lok Sabha MPs may quit party
articles.description: Following the departure of seven Rajya Sabha MPs, a senior Shiromani Akali Dal leader claims two AAP Lok Sabha MPs may also leave the party. This follows recent shifts in the Rajya Sabha where AAP members aligned with the BJP, strengthening the NDA's position. Speculation over further exits continues.

articles.title: Portuguese proverb of the day: “A barking dog does not bite” – life lessons on human nature, fear, psychology and why actions matter more than words
articles.description: Portuguese proverb of the day: “A barking dog does not bite,” highlights the difference between words and real action in human behavior. The timeless wisdom suggests that people who make loud threats, strong claims, or aggressive statements are often less likely to act on them. The proverb teaches an important life lesson about understanding intentions beyond appearances and not being intimidated by empty words.

articles.title: Encounter specialist now Bengal poll observer: Who is Ajay Pal Sharma?
articles.description: An IPS officer from the 2011 batch, Pal is currently posted as the Joint Commissioner of Police (Law and Order) in Uttar Pradesh's Prayagraj.

articles.title: AAP MLA Mehraj Malik released from Kathua jail after HC quashes PSA case
articles.description: Jammu and Kashmir Aam Aadmi Party president Mehraj Malik was released from Kathua Jail. The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court quashed the Public Safety Act case against him. Supporters welcomed Malik with joy. The High Court set aside the detention order passed by the Deputy Commissioner. This decision came after concluding all proceedings of the case.

articles.title: ‘90 Minutes Behind Closed Doors’: Araghchi Reveals 'Constructive 'Putin Talks Amid Iran Crisis
articles.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held extended talks with Russian President Vladimir Putin in Saint Petersburg covering war, regional tensions and future strategic cooperation. Araghchi called the meeting highly constructive and said Putin praised the Iranian people for resisting the United States and prevailing in an unjust war. Tehran thanked Moscow for wartime support and pledged deeper strategic ties under new conditions. Russia also promised efforts toward Middle East peace.#Putin #Iran #Russia #Araghchi #BreakingNews #WorldNews #MiddleEast #Geopolitics #Tehran #Moscow #SaintPetersburg #RussiaIran #LiveNews #PutinNews #IranNews

articles.title: 'Will I get justice?': Arvind Kejriwal poses 2 sharp questions on Delhi high court judge’s neutrality in excise case
articles.description: Arvind Kejriwal will boycott excise case hearings before Delhi HC judge Swarana Kanta Sharma, citing concerns over justice perception and potential conflict of interest. He stated his hope for justice has been shattered, choosing Satyagraha over appearing in her court. This decision follows the judge's rejection of his recusal plea.

articles.title: Why Shekhawati’s iconic havelis, once home to Birlas, Dalmias, Khaitans, are facing an existential crisis
articles.description: Rajasthan's historic Shekhawati region, famed for its fresco-painted havelis, faces severe deterioration due to neglect and demolition. Once symbols of merchant wealth, these architectural marvels are rapidly vanishing, impacting cultural heritage and tourism. Urgent preservation efforts, including government funding and community involvement, are crucial to save this 'world's largest open-air art gallery'.
